actualExecutionPerformed: Loaded the real UCI Dry Bean dataset with ucimlrepo, wrote curated CSV forms, ran schema_provenance_auditor, ran metric_stress_validator, trained dummy/logistic/random-forest baselines, ran shuffled-label and split/seed stress checks, attempted a class-weight extension, and replayed the toolchain in a network-off container.
disclaimer: Sovryn produces autonomous open-research artifacts, defensive publications, and open-source research evidence. It is not a patent filing system and does not provide legal patentability, legal novelty, or freedom-to-operate opinions.
hardQuestionAnswer: Yes, Sovryn used its self-built schema/provenance and metric-stress tools on one external scientific target. The tools revealed 68 duplicate full/feature rows, no missing/sentinel values, low shuffled-label control performance, stable but not official split-family replay, and that a class-weight extension did not improve macro-F1 on the primary split.
negativeOrPartialFindings: No official benchmark protocol was reproduced; this is a controlled baseline study.
The dataset has no official train/test split in this run, so split sensitivity remains a limitation.
schema_provenance_auditor needed Batch 9 wrapper context to identify the target column and class distribution.
Any class-weight extension claim is bounded to measured macro-F1 on one split and replay seed.
nextResearchDirection: Run the next deep target on another multiclass tabular dataset with an official or paper-defined split if available, then compare duplicate-aware and class-risk reporting across datasets.

optionalToolNotUsed.pytest_repro_summary: Dataset target only; no repository test evidence involved.

rejectedAlternatives: UCI Rice Cammeo/Osmancik: useful fallback but binary and less demanding for multiclass metric stress.
UCI Breast Cancer Wisconsin Diagnostic: measurable and public, but not selected because Dry Bean is the safer agricultural tabular target.
UCI Optical Recognition of Handwritten Digits: strong benchmark but less aligned with Batch 8.5 dataset/provenance direction for tabular agricultural data.
title: Batch 9 UCI Dry Bean Deep Toolchain Study
whatSovrynLearned: The self-built toolchain adds value beyond normal sklearn execution by forcing duplicate/provenance evidence, shuffled-label controls, seed/split stress, and a measured rejection of a class-weight extension instead of accepting a headline benchmark score.
